channel.title: cbtcbook.com — Articles on Communications-Based Train Control
channel.description: Technical articles on CBTC procurement, agency case studies, standards, and operations. Drawn from twenty years of US deployment experience.

item.title: Inside the CBTC Braking Curve: How Safe Stopping Distance Is Calculated
item.description: The kinematic physics, gradient and adhesion mechanics, and reaction-time budgets that turn v² = u² + 2as into the Emergency Braking Curve every Communications-Based Train Control train obeys 20 times a second.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: How to Write a CBTC RFP That Doesn't Lock You Into One Vendor
item.description: A practical framework for US transit agencies drafting a Communications-Based Train Control procurement that preserves competition for the next 30 years. Drawn from twenty years of US deployment experience.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: NYC MTA L Line CBTC: 25 Years of Lessons (2001–2026)
item.description: A practitioner's read of the L Line CBTC project from the 1997 RFP through 2026 operations: Siemens Trainguard MT, the 2006 partial revenue, the 2009 full transition, Hurricane Sandy, and what the rest of US transit owes to it.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: Siemens Trainguard MT vs Alstom Urbalis: Architecture Compared
item.description: A neutral architectural comparison of Siemens Trainguard MT and Alstom Urbalis CBTC platforms, oriented to US transit agency procurement decisions.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang

item.title: What Is Communications-Based Train Control (CBTC)? A 2026 Plain-English Guide
item.description: Communications-Based Train Control (CBTC) explained for US transit professionals: what it is, how it works, why agencies adopt it, and where it stands in 2026.
item.author: Chunjun (Francisco) Wang
